description: Address code review findings: webhook signature verification, admin route gating, rate limiter documentation, KYC flow wiring, escrow concurrency guard, and RLS helper fallback.
steps: Fix Stripe webhook to compute HMAC-SHA256 signature using Node.js crypto module
Fix PayPal webhook to implement proper certificate-based verification pattern
Fix middleware admin route gating fail-open logic and add server-side role check utility
Add development/single-instance documentation comment to rate limiter and TODO for Redis. Wire rate limiter in middleware for API routes.
Wire verification-flow.tsx handleSubmit to call server actions from verification-actions.ts
Add SELECT ... FOR UPDATE in process_escrow_release function in 004_functions.sql
Add COALESCE fallback in the get_user_extended_id() helper in 003_rls_policies.sql
acceptance_criteria: Webhook routes properly verify signatures using crypto HMAC
Middleware denies admin access when cookie is absent
Rate limiter has clear documentation about limitations and a TODO for Redis
RLS helper handles missing users_extended row
findings: All 6 actionable review issues addressed. Build and lint pass cleanly. Issue 7 (no tests) was documented as acceptable for initial scaffold - no action taken. The PayPal webhook verification requires the PAYPAL_WEBHOOK_ID env var which was added to .env.example. The RLS helper uses a sentinel UUID (all zeros) as fallback which will never match any real row, effectively denying access until the trigger creates the users_extended row (which happens immediately on signup via the new trigger).
